topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model – states of matter, particle arrangement, motion, forces between particles, and basic thermal behavior

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Uses a temperature-time graph for two pure substances (X and Y) to identify and compare their respective melting points while in the solid state.
questions.reasoning_focus: Interpreting the plateau on a heating curve to determine state transition temperatures.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Asks which macroscopic property of a substance (ability to flow, compressibility, shape, or volume) remains unchanged when it transitions from a liquid to a gas.
questions.reasoning_focus: Identifying the consistent physical properties across different states of matter.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Compares the compressibility of iron and oxygen at room temperature to explain the difference based on particle proximity.
questions.reasoning_focus: Correlating the distance between particles in solids versus gases with their macroscopic resistance to compression.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Explains why the pressure of a gas in a syringe decreases when the volume is increased at a constant temperature, using the frequency of particle collisions.
questions.reasoning_focus: Explaining macroscopic pressure changes through the microscopic behavior of gas particles striking container walls.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Describes evaporation as the process where more-energetic molecules escape from the surface of a liquid.
questions.reasoning_focus: Identifying how the distribution of energy among molecules leads to surface cooling and phase transition.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Compares the separation and speed of particles in a hot gas versus a cool liquid at identical pressure.
questions.reasoning_focus: Understanding how temperature and physical state simultaneously affect particle distance and velocity.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Identifies the specific particles (individual molecules) that escape from a water surface during evaporation.
questions.reasoning_focus: Defining the microscopic components involved in the transition from liquid to gas.

questions.topic: Thermal Physics and the Particle Model
questions.question_summary: Explains the increase in pressure of a heated gas in a sealed container based on the forces exerted by particles striking the walls.
questions.reasoning_focus: Relating thermal energy increases to the force of individual particle collisions against a boundary.
